mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative median forward returns or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when inflation exceeds 3% annually or during documented supply chain disruption periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth above 2.5 percent year-over-year forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 65% of high-inflation trigger months or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions in a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate confirms robust job creation absorbing labor supply, which sustains wage growth and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction signal yields negative median returns or loses statistical significance when tested against a univariate payroll model in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and corporate profit momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows or positive return frequency falls below 50% during confirmed expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals abrupt manufacturing contraction and inventory destocking, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.
